message:
chore(deps): regenerate Cargo.lock with registry source/checksum for fsqlite family

The previous Cargo.lock entries for the fsqlite workspace crates
(fsqlite, fsqlite-ast, fsqlite-btree, fsqlite-func, fsqlite-pager,
fsqlite-parser, fsqlite-planner, fsqlite-types, fsqlite-vdbe,
fsqlite-vfs, fsqlite-wal) carried no `source` or `checksum` lines,
which cargo emits for unpublished path/git dependencies. The current
Cargo.toml already pins these to registry versions (e.g.
`fsqlite = "0.1.2"`, `fsqlite-vfs = "0.1.3"`), so the lockfile needs
matching
`source = "registry+https://github.com/rust-lang/crates.io-index"`
plus checksum entries for reproducible builds against crates.io.

Cargo automatically reconciled the lockfile against the registry on
the next resolve, producing this diff:

  - Adds `source` + `checksum` to all 11 fsqlite-family packages.
  - Drops transitive dev/intra-crate deps the registry-published
    0.1.2 tarballs no longer need at resolve time (e.g. fsqlite-btree
    no longer brings in fsqlite-func + serde, fsqlite-planner drops
    lru + xxhash-rust, fsqlite-types drops memchr, fsqlite-vfs drops
    smallvec). These reflect the published crate's actual dep
    surface, not a semantic change here.
  - Introduces a second lru entry (0.12.5) alongside the existing
    0.16.4 because one of the registry-published fsqlite crates pins
    the older lru. The application-level `lru` dep in the ratatui
    chain stays on 0.16.4 — this is a standard diamond.

Cargo.toml is unchanged; this is a pure lockfile reconciliation. Pins
beads_rust to build against the published crates.io artifacts rather
than a stale path-dep cache.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
